title: Quadlet - support oneshot .kube files
body:
Allow users to manually set the Service Type
Add test
Update README

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?
Yes

```release-note
Quadlet - allow users to set the service type of a .kube file to oneshot to support yaml files without containers
```

comments.body: Ephemeral COPR build failed. @containers/packit-build please check.
Cockpit tests failed for commit fade91ed323a15e615724df5297027db8bd1e61b. @martinpitt, @jelly, @mvollmer please check.
Ephemeral COPR build failed. @containers/packit-build please check.

> I am not yet 100% on board with supporting that for containers. I would recommend setting `Restart=no/never`.

`oneshot` is already supported for `.container` files: https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/pkg/systemd/quadlet/quadlet.go#L477. I merely copied the same behavior.
As for `.kube` files, Since we cannot block users from using K8S Yamls without containers, this issue is bound to rise at some point. Obviously, we can say it is not supported, but I don't see a reason why.

> I also think we need system tests for it to make sure `oneshot` doesn't cause issues with the sd-notify policies of containers etc.

I'll add one

> `oneshot` is already supported for `.container` files: https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/pkg/systemd/quadlet/quadlet.go#L477. I merely copied the same behavior.

I had no idea :) Thanks!

> As for `.kube` files, Since we cannot block users from using K8S Yamls without containers, this issue is bound to rise at some point. Obviously, we can say it is not supported, but I don't see a reason why.

Totally agree.  I get why it makes for `.kube` but less for `.container`.  But since it's already allowed, I am good.

FTR, cockpit test failures are all due to #20342 . If this is still open by Monday, I'll add a "naughty pattern" for it, i.e. ignore the failure, as it causes too much noise (but still, let's please not release that).
